key_highlights: Tech sector selloff led by Korean semiconductor stocks and Broadcom earnings weighing on markets, snapping S&P 500's 9-day win streak.
US-Iran tensions and Middle East conflict escalate, pressuring oil prices and fueling risk-off sentiment.
May jobs report expected to show moderate growth amid persistent inflation, a key catalyst for Fed policy.
AI trade shows signs of fatigue with Anthropic calling for a pause and Robinhood's new AI trader raising red flags.
SpaceX IPO at $1.77 trillion valuation banned for Chinese/HK investors, highlighting geopolitical tech decoupling.
Emerging markets under pressure: Indonesia crisis of confidence, Korean tech rout, India growth beats but fuel shocks loom.
Logistics costs surge to four-year high, adding to inflation concerns and pressuring the Fed.
Rivian extends 10-day rally ahead of R2 launch; GameStop jumps 6% on stellar earnings.
Oil industry warns drained inventories could spike gasoline prices; gas dips 17 cents nationally.
Private market jitters return as Partners Group caps withdrawals; Wellington buys Hartford Funds for $1.9B.

sectors.trend: Broad selloff led by Korean tech and Broadcom earnings disappointment; chip rally cools. TSMC warns supply won't meet AI demand for years, but ASML becomes biggest European company. Sandisk and Western Digital hit all-time highs.
sectors.implications: Near-term volatility in semis, but long-term AI demand remains robust. Opportunities in memory/storage and AI infrastructure plays.

sectors.trend: Oil slips on US-Iran talks but tensions remain high; logistics costs surge to four-year high. Gasoline prices dip 17 cents nationally but oil industry warns of potential spike from drained inventories. Delfin approves $5B FID for LNG export vessel.
sectors.implications: Energy sector remains volatile with geopolitical risk. Logistics inflation pressures Fed. LNG and midstream offer long-term opportunities.

sectors.trend: Korean tech selloff drags EM stocks; Indonesia faces crisis of confidence; India growth beats estimates but fuel shocks loom; Philippines inflation slows; China/HK investors banned from SpaceX IPO.
sectors.implications: EMs are under pressure from tech rout and geopolitical tensions. India remains a bright spot but faces consumption headwinds. Selective opportunities in India and ASEAN.

sectors.trend: Bernstein sours on packaged food stocks due to inflation and GLP-1 drugs; Macy's turnaround plan shows promise; all-inclusive travel trend heats up; Rivian extends rally ahead of R2 launch.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary is mixed: travel and EV demand strong, but packaged food faces headwinds. Retail turnaround stories like Macy's offer selective upside.

sectors.trend: Moderna climbs 7.5% ahead of key updates; Medtronic jumps 5.7% on stellar Q4; BDX underperforms healthcare sector; AI doctors push gains traction.
sectors.implications: Healthcare shows strength with earnings beats and AI integration. Biotech remains event-driven. Long-term AI in medicine is a transformative theme.

sectors.trend: Private market jitters return as Partners Group caps withdrawals; Wellington buys Hartford Funds for $1.9B; Bill Ackman's Pershing Square set to make $600M on Universal stake; Robinhood's AI trader raises red flags.
sectors.implications: Private markets face liquidity concerns. Asset management M&A continues. AI in trading is controversial but growing. Financials remain stable.

sectors.trend: Sydney home listings hit 17-year high, Melbourne 12-year peak; Tishman Speyer secures $300M for Korea living venture; TransAlta buys Colorado gas plants for $1B.
sectors.implications: Housing supply increases in Australia, giving buyers power. Infrastructure and energy transition deals remain active.

key_themes: AI trade fatigue and regulatory scrutiny (Anthropic pause, Robinhood AI concerns, CEO raises diverted to AI)
Geopolitical risk: US-Iran tensions, Middle East conflict, US-China tech decoupling (SpaceX IPO ban)
Inflation persistence: logistics costs surge, fuel shocks, Fed policy uncertainty ahead of jobs report
Emerging market divergence: India growth vs. Indonesia/Korea weakness
Private market liquidity concerns and M&A activity
Consumer resilience in travel and EVs, but packaged food under pressure
Semiconductor supply-demand imbalance: TSMC warns of multi-year shortage
Energy transition: LNG, solar, graphene, and carbon capture deals

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy and defense stocks; hedge with gold and commodities; reduce EM exposure in conflict zones.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech sector correction (semiconductor selloff, AI fatigue)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify within tech; focus on AI infrastructure and memory/storage; avoid overvalued AI hype stocks; consider defensive tech like cybersecurity.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Inflation persistence and Fed policy tightening
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor TIPS, commodities, and value stocks; reduce duration in bonds; monitor logistics costs and wage data.
Underweight EM equities except India; focus on domestic-demand-driven stories; avoid tech-heavy EM indices.
Reduce allocation to illiquid private funds; increase cash reserves; monitor redemption gates and NAV adjustments.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and ethical risks in AI
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid companies with high regulatory exposure; favor established AI players with diversified revenue; monitor Anthropic and Robinhood developments.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Energy supply shock (oil inventory drain, gasoline spike)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase energy sector allocation; hedge with oil futures; reduce exposure to consumer discretionary sensitive to fuel costs.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure and memory/storage plays (e.g., Sandisk, Western Digital, TSMC, ASML)
opportunities.rationale: TSMC warns of multi-year supply shortage; memory stocks hit all-time highs; AI demand remains robust despite near-term volatility.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Indian equities on dips (e.g., Hindustan Zinc, domestic demand plays)
opportunities.rationale: India growth beats estimates, bond tax cut eases access for global funds, but fuel shocks pose near-term risk. Long-term structural story intact.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy transition and LNG infrastructure (e.g., Delfin, TransAlta, First Graphene)
opportunities.rationale: Delfin approves $5B FID for LNG; TransAlta buys gas plants; graphene acquisition expands US footprint. Energy security and decarbonization drive demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Rivian on pullbacks ahead of R2 launch
opportunities.rationale: Rivian extends 10-day rally; R2 launch is a major catalyst. EV demand remains strong, and Rivian is well-positioned in the pickup/SUV segment.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider defensive healthcare (Medtronic, Moderna) on earnings momentum
opportunities.rationale: Medtronic and Moderna posted strong earnings; AI in medicine is a growing theme. Healthcare offers defensive growth amid market volatility.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash and short-duration bonds ahead of jobs report and Fed meeting
defensive_moves.rationale: May jobs report is a gut check for bond traders; logistics costs surge and inflation remains sticky. Cash provides optionality.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with gold and commodities against geopolitical and inflation risks
defensive_moves.rationale: US-Iran tensions, oil inventory drain, and logistics inflation support gold and commodity exposure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to Korean tech and EM equities (except India)
defensive_moves.rationale: Korean tech selloff, Indonesia crisis, and China/HK ban on SpaceX IPO signal headwinds. Rotate to US and India.
Inflation and GLP-1 drugs are pressuring margins and demand. Avoid CPG names with high exposure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor private market allocations and consider reducing illiquid holdings
defensive_moves.rationale: Partners Group caps withdrawals; private market jitters return. Increase liquidity buffer.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile in the next 1-3 months due to geopolitical tensions (US-Iran), tech sector correction, and uncertainty ahead of the May jobs report and Fed policy. The S&P 500 snapped a 9-day win streak, and the AI trade is showing fatigue. Expect choppy trading with a defensive tilt. Oil prices and logistics costs will be key inflation watchpoints.
market_outlook.long_term: Over 6-12 months, the outlook is cautiously optimistic. AI infrastructure spending (TSMC, ASML, memory) and energy transition (LNG, solar) provide structural growth. India and select EM markets offer diversification. However, persistent inflation, Fed tightening, and geopolitical risks could cap upside. The SpaceX IPO and AI regulatory developments will be major themes. A balanced portfolio with growth and defensive exposure is recommended.
key_catalysts: May US jobs report (June 5, 2026) – key for Fed rate path
SpaceX IPO (potential largest ever) – regulatory and market impact
AI regulation developments (Anthropic pause, Robinhood AI scrutiny)
Rivian R2 launch and EV demand data
TSMC supply chain updates and chip demand outlook
India budget and fuel price adjustments
Oil inventory data and gasoline price trends
Private market liquidity events (Partners Group, Wellington deal)
watch_list: S&P 500 and Nasdaq technical levels (support at 50-day MA)
10-year Treasury yield and breakeven inflation rates
Emerging market currency and bond flows (especially Indonesia, India)
Semiconductor index (SOX) and AI-related ETF flows
Logistics cost indices (e.g., Cass Freight Index)
Consumer confidence and retail sales data
Corporate earnings from Broadcom, Nvidia, and other tech bellwethers
Geopolitical headlines from Middle East and US-China relations
